artifacts.claimText: SKKU maintains a ChatGPT Total Information site that introduces ChatGPT and frames it as a tool that can be applied across fields while requiring careful educational use because it may provide nonexistent information.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Sungkyunkwan University ( SKKU ) chatGPT Total Information | Introducing ChatGPT
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: However, a notable weakness of ChatGPT is pointed out as its tendency to provide nonexistent information as if it exists. This can be problematic when it is used as an educational tool in schools, so careful use is needed.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: SKKU explains that ChatGPT can be applied across fields but warns that it may provide nonexistent information, so careful educational use is needed.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow guidance claim supported by an SKKU-hosted source snapshot; not marked canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: SKKU student guidance cautions against relying solely on AI detection software when ChatGPT abuse is suspected and says professors should consider class participation, previous assignments, interviews, or real-time re-evaluations to prevent unfairness.

citation.citationTitle: Sungkyunkwan University (SKKU) AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Sungkyunkwan University ( SKKU ) chatGPT Total Information | Student Use Cases
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is necessary to discuss educational use in AI and guide them how to use it in a correct way, rather than suspecting their assignments based solely on AI detection software. If ChatGPT abusing is suspected, professors should consider various factors, such as the student's class participation, previous assignments, and provide assistance through interviews or real-time re-evaluations to prevent unfairness.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: SKKU guidance cautions against suspicion based solely on AI detection software and points to contextual review methods.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Student Use Cases; case study discussion

artifacts.title: Sungkyunkwan University (SKKU) staged AI guidance review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found SKKU-hosted ChatGPT Total Information guidance pages. The evidence supports narrow claims about guidance, listed tool-use examples, AI-detection caution, and instructor teaching strategies, but not a formal university-wide binding AI policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
SKKU sources found in this run are guidance-style ChatGPT information pages; no formal binding university-wide generative-AI policy was identified.
The HWP KingoGPT press attachment was not used as evidence because this run did not extract reliable original text from the binary file.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
